learningPromise: A self-contained component catalog for the Mermaid course renderer.
diagram:
graph LR
  C[Concept] --> D1[Diagram 1]
  D1 --> S1[Storyboard 1]
  S1 --> S2[Storyboard 2]
  S2 --> D2[Diagram 2]
  D2 --> Z[Zoomable diagram]

units.title: Concept Unit
units.body: Concept units are quiet prose blocks for setting context before a visual or code-heavy interaction. This fixture keeps the paragraph long enough to exercise typography, line height, and width without turning into a real lesson.

units.title: Request Flow
units.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[Client] --> B[Server]
  B --> C[Client]
units.caption: A simple request-response cycle.

units.title: Command Palette Storyboard
units.caption: A multi-scene storyboard showing how the command palette is built and used.

scenes.name: Filter
scenes.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[Profiles] --> B[Filter active]
  B --> C[Commands]
scenes.explanation: Live profile data determines which commands exist.

code.source:
const commands = profiles
  .filter((profile) => profile.name !== active)
  .map((profile, index) => ({
    id: `activate-${profile.name}`,
    label: `Activate ${profile.name}`,
    shortcut: index < 3 ? `⌘${index + 1}` : undefined,
    category: 'Profile',
    action: () => activate(profile.name),
  }))

highlights.note: Filter out the currently active profile.
Only the first three commands get keyboard shortcuts.

scenes.name: Navigate
scenes.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  D[Navigation] --> E[Merge]
  C[Commands] --> E
  E --> F[Palette]
scenes.explanation: Navigation commands merge with generated commands for a unified palette.

units.title: Dispatcher Flow
units.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[Unit] --> B{kind?}
  B --> C[concept]
  B --> D[storyboard]
  B --> E[diagram]
  C --> F[Prose]
  D --> G[Cinema Strip]
  E --> H[Figure]
units.caption: The dispatcher routes each unit kind to its specialized renderer.

units.question: If the right column scrolls, which code lines should stay highlighted?

reveal.explanation: The active card is the source of truth. The code panel simply mirrors its line list.

units.title: Compare Unit
units.lesson: Compare units make a contrast visible. The code blocks share the same numbered renderer as storyboards, so line treatment stays consistent.

units.title: Zoomable Diagram
units.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[Source data] --> B[Renderer]
  B --> C[Component]
  C --> D[Interaction]
  D --> E[E2E selector]
units.caption: A diagram story exercises Mermaid rendering, active-node contrast, and the zoom overlay controls.

units.title: Renderer Pipeline
units.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[Fixture data] --> B[PAGE object]
  B --> C[Unit list]
  C --> D[renderUnit]
  D --> E[DOM stories]
  E --> F[data-story-id]
units.caption: The renderer pipeline from fixture data to stable DOM selectors.

units.title: Surprise Unit
units.body: The story page is also a contract. When a component changes, the fixture should make that change obvious before it leaks into generated course pages.
Use this page as the renderer's living component inventory. It is intentionally small, self-contained, and selector-friendly, so manual review and e2e coverage can share the same target.
